question: This area chart shows average test scores for different school subjects. What is the most appropriate conclusion we can draw from this visualization?

options.A: The chart implies a smooth progression in performance from one subject to the next, suggesting the subjects lie on a single continuous difficulty scale.
options.B: Art has the highest average score among the subjects shown.
options.C: The chart demonstrates a clear upward time trend in scores across the school year.
options.D: Small score changes between neighboring subjects are more meaningful than differences between subjects that are far apart on the x-axis.

explanation: This area chart joins category values for subject into a continuous filled region. However, the x‑axis categories (e.g., English, Geography, Math, PE, ...) have no inherent numeric or spatial continuity, so the apparent smooth trend is an artifact of the chart type rather than a meaningful pattern. A bar chart would allow readers to compare each category independently without implying a continuous progression.
